title: Retrieval Review: Accessible HTML Engineering
summary: Retrieval Review: Accessible HTML Engineering retrieves prior skills in an original changed civic-service accessibility release scenario and requires implementation, behavior, failure-state, and transfer evidence.
objectives: Apply accessibility disability models, accessibility tree, keyboard focus, accessible names descriptions, aria use, accessible errors status, accessible media equivalents, accessibility test strategy in unfamiliar constraints.
Produce or audit a real changed civic-service accessibility release artifact without happy-path-only reasoning.
Preserve earlier requirements while testing keyboard, narrow-width, invalid, and assistive-semantic states.

starterFiles.html: <!-- Continue and harden the Community Needs Survey artifact here. -->

steps.title: 01 · predict 1: extreme-heat service barrier review · review
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 1: Which claim survives the changed case? Scenario: changed civic-service accessibility release.
steps.why: Retrieval Review: Accessible HTML Engineering makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 1 in changed civic-service accessibility release.

content.title: changed civic-service accessibility release evidence
content.text: Choose the claim that preserves purpose, native behavior, and understandable relationships when presentation changes.

hints: Retrieval Review: Accessible HTML Engineering checkpoint 1: identify the user task and data relationship first.
Retrieval Review: Accessible HTML Engineering checkpoint 1: compare source, native behavior, and announced meaning.
Retrieval Review: Accessible HTML Engineering checkpoint 1: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: Changed civic-service accessibility release case 1: accessibility is a niche feature for a small fixed category of users.
Changed civic-service accessibility release case 1: trace each task through varied input, perception, comprehension, motion, and environmental conditions, then remove the barrier at its source.
Changed civic-service accessibility release case 1: build one separate accessible version after the standard product is complete.

options.text: changed civic-service accessibility release phase 2.1: select a representative interactive element in the DOM
changed civic-service accessibility release phase 2.2: predict its name, role, state, description, and tree position
changed civic-service accessibility release phase 2.3: inspect the computed accessibility properties and rendered exposure
changed civic-service accessibility release phase 2.4: repair the earliest source relationship and confirm the announcement

options.text: Changed civic-service accessibility release case 3: adding positive tabindex values repairs any keyboard sequence.
Changed civic-service accessibility release case 3: keep DOM and visual order aligned, use native controls, preserve visible focus, and test every task without a pointer.
Changed civic-service accessibility release case 3: remove focus outlines after mouse use because keyboard users can remember position.

options.text: Changed civic-service accessibility release case 5: aRIA attributes automatically implement keyboard interaction and state changes.
Changed civic-service accessibility release case 5: start with native HTML, use the smallest ARIA addition for an actual gap, and verify semantics plus complete behavior over time.
Changed civic-service accessibility release case 5: replace every button with a div role button so visual styling is easier.

options.text: Changed civic-service accessibility release case 6: color and a red border give every learner enough error information.

Changed civic-service accessibility release case 6: summarize what failed, connect specific text to each invalid field, move focus deliberately, preserve input, and announce status at the right urgency.
Changed civic-service accessibility release case 6: use assertive live regions for every keystroke so no change can be missed.

options.text: changed civic-service accessibility release phase 7.1: inventory speech, meaningful sound, visual-only action, text, and interactive controls

changed civic-service accessibility release phase 7.3: review timing, speakers, terminology, reading order, and control access
changed civic-service accessibility release phase 7.4: complete the same information task with sound off, visuals unavailable, and keyboard only

Changed civic-service accessibility release case 8: test one browser and viewport because accessibility APIs normalize every environment.
